---
title: "Tests for data-generating mechanisms"
author: "Stacy Cyrille"
date: last-modified
format: 
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  # html:
  #   keep-md: false
  #   toc: true
  #   toc-location: left
  #   number-sections: true
  #   embed-resources: true
execute: 
  # echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
fig-height: 6
fig-align: "center"
fig-cap-location: top
html-math-method: mathml
---

\newpage

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(MASS)
library(knitr)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(timereg)
library(gtsummary)

source(here::here("R", "utils.R"))
source(here::here("R", "generate_data.R"))
source(here::here("R", "performance_eval.R"))

options(scipen = 999)
```

\newpage

# Simulate binary variables  

**Example 1**

We generate a simple example.

-   Sample size: $n = 1000$
-   Number of DNA pathways: $p = 3$
-   Prevalence of DNA pathways: $\pi_1 = 0.1, \quad \pi_2 = 0.4, \quad \pi_3 = 0.6 \quad$
-   Correlation matrix:

$$\Sigma = \left(
\begin{array}{ccc}
\text{1} & \text{0} & \text{0.2} \\
\text{0} & \text{1} & \text{0.5} \\
\text{0.2} & \text{0.5} & \text{1}
\end{array}
\right)$$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)

n <- 1000
prev <- c(0.1, 0.4, 0.6)

Sigma <- matrix(c(1,   0,   0.2,
                  0,   1,   0.5,
                  0.2, 0.5, 1), 
                3, 3)

dna1 <- data.frame(simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma))
head(dna1)

# Check prevalences
colMeans(dna1)

# Check dependence (Fisher’s exact tests)
fisher.test(table(dna1$X1, dna1$X2))
fisher.test(table(dna1$X1, dna1$X3))
fisher.test(table(dna1$X2, dna1$X3))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Example 2**

We generate only uncorrelated variables.

-   Sample size: $$n = 1000$$
-   Number of DNA pathways: $$p = 3$$
-   Prevalence of DNA pathways: $\pi_1 = 0.1, \quad \pi_2 = 0.4, \quad \pi_3 = 0.6 \quad$
-   Correlation matrix:

$$\Sigma = \left(
\begin{array}{ccc}
\text{1} & \text{0} & \text{0} \\
\text{0} & \text{1} & \text{0} \\
\text{0} & \text{0} & \text{1}
\end{array}
\right)$$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
Sigma0 <- diag(3)
dna0 <- data.frame(simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma0))

# Check prevalences
colMeans(dna0)

# Check dependence (Fisher’s exact tests)
fisher.test(table(dna0$X1, dna0$X2))
fisher.test(table(dna0$X1, dna0$X3))
fisher.test(table(dna0$X2, dna0$X3))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Example 3**

We simulate correlated variables grouped into blocks.

-   Sample size: $n = 1000$
-   Number of DNA pathways: $p = 20$
-   Prevalence of DNA pathways: $\pi \sim \mathcal{U}(0.1, 0.6)$
-   Block size: $b = 5$
-   Number of blocks: $K = 4$
-   Within-block correlations: $\rho_{k1} = 0.6, \quad \rho_{k2} = 0.5, \quad \rho_{k3} = 0.4, \quad \rho_{k4} = 0.1$
-   Between-block correlation: $\rho_{B} = 0.05$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
p <- 20 

# Generate random prevalences 
prev <- runif(20, 0.1, 0.6)
prev
# Initialize the matrix
Sigma <- generate_block_corr(p, 
                             block_size = 5, 
                             rho_within = c(0.6, 0.5, 0.4, 0.1), 
                             rho_between = 0.05)
# Inspect the matrix 
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:p)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:p)
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("X",1:p)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("X",1:p)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

# Simulate correlated data using this matrix 
dna_block <- data.frame(simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma))

# Check marginal prevalences
data.frame(true_prev = prev, 
           est_prev = colMeans(dna_block))%>%
  rownames_to_column("variable")%>%
  kable(format = "html", caption ="True and empirical prevalences", 
        col.names = c("Variable","True prevalence","Estimated prevalence"))

# 20x20 matrix of raw p-values
pval_block <- pairwise_fisher(dna_block, names(dna_block))

# Heatmap of raw p-values
mat_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(pval_block$p.value.matrix))%>%
  mutate(Freq = ifelse(Var1==Var2, NA, Freq))
ggplot(mat_long, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", direction = -1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(fill = "Raw p-value", x ="", y = "", 
       title = "Heatmap of raw p-values")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

All simulations were repeated across $R=100$ replications. 

For each replicate, pairwise associations between DNA-based pathways were tested using Fisher's exact test, and the empirical type I error was estimated as the proportion of replications with p-values below the nominal significance level ($\alpha=0.05$)

We simulate correlated variables grouped into blocks.

* Sample size: $n = 1000$
* Number of DNA pathways: $p = 20$
* Prevalence of DNA pathways: $\pi \sim \mathcal{U}(0.1, 0.6)$
* Block size: $b = 5$
* Number of blocks: $K = 4$
* Within-block correlations: $\rho_{k1} = 0.6, \quad \rho_{k2} = 0.5, \quad \rho_{k3} = 0.4, \quad \rho_{k4} = 0.05$
* Between-block correlation: $\rho_{B} = 0.01$

```{r eval=FALSE}
set.seed(2026)
R <- 1000
n <- 1000
p <- 20
prev <- runif(p, 0.1, 0.6) %>%
  set_names(paste0("X",1:p))
Sigma <- generate_block_corr(p, 
                             block_size = 5, 
                             rho_within = c(0.6, 0.5, 0.4, 0.05),
                             rho_between = 0.01)

# Inspect the matrix 
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:20)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:20)
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("D",1:20)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("D",1:20)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

# Run simulations
dna_sims <- replicate(R,
                      simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma),
                      simplify = FALSE) %>%
  imap(~ mutate(data.frame(.x), R = .y)) %>%
  bind_rows()

# Prevalence bias 
dna_sims %>%
  bind_rows()%>%
  pivot_longer(-R, names_to = "pathway", values_to = "est_prev") %>%
  dplyr::full_join(rownames_to_column(data.frame(true_prev = prev), 
                                      "pathway"), by ="pathway")%>%
  group_by(pathway) %>%
  summarize(bias = estimate_bias(true = true_prev, est = est_prev),
            pathway = factor(pathway, levels = paste0("D", 1:20)),
            .groups = "drop")%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(x = pathway, y = bias, colour = pathway))+
      geom_point(aes(fill = "pathway"))+
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed")+
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 0.05))+
      labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Bias", title = "Prevalence bias")+
      theme_minimal()+
      theme(legend.position = "none")
  }

# Type I error 
dna_sims_pval <- dna_sims %>%
  group_split(R) %>%
  map(~.x %>% 
        dplyr::select(-R)%>%
        pairwise_fisher(vars = names(.)))

# Heatmap of mean raw p-values
dna_sims_pval %>%
  map(~as.data.frame(as.table(.x$p.value.matrix))%>%
        mutate(Freq = ifelse(Var1==Var2, NA, Freq)))%>%
  reduce(bind_rows, .id = "R")%>%
  group_by(Var1, Var2)%>%
  summarise(mean_p.value = mean(Freq), .groups = "drop")%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(Var1, Var2, fill = mean_p.value)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", direction = -1) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Mean raw p-value", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Heatmap of mean raw p-values")
  }

# Heatmap of the proportion of replications with p-values < 0.05
dna_sims_pval %>%
  map(~as.data.frame(as.table(.x$p.value.matrix))%>%
        mutate(Freq = ifelse(Var1==Var2, NA, Freq)))%>%
  reduce(bind_rows, .id = "R")%>%
  group_by(Var1, Var2)%>%
  summarise(mean_typeI = mean(Freq < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), 
            .groups = "drop")%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(Var1, Var2, fill = mean_typeI)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", direction = -1) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Proportion of replications \nwith p-values < 0.05", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Heatmap of the proportion of replications with p-values < 0.05")
  }
```

\newpage

# Simulate continuous variables 

**Example 1**

We generate a simple example. 

* Sample size: $n = 1000$
* Number of RNA pathways: $p = 3$
* Correlation matrix: 

$$\Sigma = \left(
\begin{array}{ccc}
\text{1} & \text{0} & \text{0.2} \\
\text{0} & \text{1} & \text{0.5} \\
\text{0.2} & \text{0.5} & \text{1}
\end{array}
\right)$$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
p <- 3 
mu <- 0
Sigma <- matrix(c(1,   0,   0.2,
                  0,   1,   -0.5,
                  0.2, -0.5, 1), 
                3, 3)
# Inspect the matrix 
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:p)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:p)
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("X",1:p)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("X",1:p)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

rna1 <- simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu, Sigma)

# Check mean 
colMeans(rna1)

# Check variance
apply(rna1, 2, var)

# Correlation matrix
as.data.frame(cor(rna1))%>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
  mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
         var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("X",1:20)),
         var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("X",1:20)))%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                           limits = c(-1,1)) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Correlation matrix of simulated variables")
  }
```

---

**Example 2**

We simulate correlated variables grouped into blocks.

* Sample size: $n = 1000$
* Number of RNA pathways $p = 20$
* Block size: $b = 5$
* Number of blocks: $K = 4$
* Within-block correlations: $\rho_{k1} = 0.7, \quad \rho_{k2} = -0.2, \quad \rho_{k3} = 0.5, \quad \rho_{k4} = 0.1$
* Between-block correlation: $\rho_{B} = 0.05$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
p = 20 

# Initialize the matrix
Sigma <- generate_block_corr(p, 
                             block_size = 5, 
                             rho_within = c(0.7, -0.2, 0.5, 0.1), 
                             rho_between = 0.05)
# Inspect the matrix 
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:20)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:20)
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("X",1:20)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("X",1:20)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

# Simulate correlated data using this matrix 
rna_block <- simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu, Sigma)

# Check mean 
colMeans(rna_block)

# Check variance 
apply(rna_block, 2, var)

# Correlation matrix
as.data.frame(cor(rna_block))%>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
  mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
         var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("X",1:20)),
         var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("X",1:20)))%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                           limits = c(-1,1)) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Correlation matrix of simulated variables")
  }
```

----------------------------------------------------

All simulations were repeated across $R=100$ replications. 

For each pathway, we computed the empirical mean, variance, and pairwise correlation coefficients of the RNA pathways across simulation replicates. 
Mean, variance and correlation coefficients were averaged across replicates. 

* Sample size: $n = 1000$
* Number of RNA pathways $p = 20$
* Block size: $b = 5$
* Number of blocks: $K = 4$
* Within-block correlations: $\rho_{k1} = 0.7, \quad \rho_{k2} = -0.2, \quad \rho_{k3} = 0.5, \quad \rho_{k4} = 0.3$
* Between-block correlation: $\rho_{B} = 0.1$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
R <- 1000
p <- 20 
mu <- 0

# Initialize the matrix
Sigma <- generate_block_corr(p, 
                             block_size = 5, 
                             rho_within = c(0.7, -0.2, 0.5, 0.3), 
                             rho_between = 0.1)
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:20)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("X",1:20)

# Run simulations 
rna_sims <- replicate(R,
                      simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu, Sigma),
                      simplify = FALSE)

# Check mean 
rna_sims %>%
  map(~colMeans(.x))%>%
  reduce(bind_rows)%>%
  summarise_all(mean)%>%
  pivot_longer(everything())%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(x = name, y = value, colour = name))+
      geom_point()+
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed")+
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.025, 0.025))+
      labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Empirical mean", 
           title = "Empirical mean across replicates")+
      theme_minimal()+
      theme(legend.position = "none")
  }

# Check variance
rna_sims %>%
  map(~ apply(.x, 2, var))%>%
  reduce(bind_rows)%>%
  summarise_all(mean)%>%
  pivot_longer(everything())%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(x = name, y = value, colour = name))+
      geom_point()+
      geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed")+
      labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Average variance", 
           title = "Average variance across replicates")+
      theme_minimal()+
      theme(legend.position = "none")
  }

# True correlation matrix 
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("X",1:20)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("X",1:20)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

# Average correlation matrix across simulation replicates
cor_sims_rna <- rna_sims %>%
  map(cor) %>%
  reduce(`+`) / length(rna_sims)

as.data.frame(cor_sims_rna)%>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
  mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
         var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("X",1:20)),
         var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("X",1:20)))%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                           limits = c(-1,1)) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Average correlation matrix across simulation replicates")
  }
```

\newpage

# Simulate time-to-event data

## Aalen additive model 

Four binary covariates were generated *independently* with prevalences spanning 
rare to frequent alterations, $X_j \sim Bernoulli(\pi_j)$ with 
$\pi_j =(0.005, 0.02, 0.05, 0.20)$. 

Events times were simulated under the following time-constant additive hazards 
model: 

$$\lambda(t|x)=\lambda_0+\beta_{1}X_{1}+\beta_{2}X_{2}+\beta_{3}X_{3}+\beta_{4}X_{4}$$
with $\lambda_0=0.05$ and $\beta=(0.06, 0.02, 0.005, 0)$. 

Independent censoring times were generated as $C \sim Uniform(0, c_{max})$. The
parameter $c_{max}$ was calibrated using a large Monte Carlo sample from the 
data-generating mechanism to achieve an expected censoring proportion of 
approximately 5%, and the calibrated value was then fixed across all 
simulation replicates. 

```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility 
set.seed(2026)

# Generate data
n <- 1000
p <- 4
sigma <- diag(x = 1, nrow = p, ncol = p)
prev <- c(0.005, 0.02, 0.05, 0.20)
beta <- c(0.06, 0.02, 0.005, 0)
lambda0 <- 0.05
target_cens <- 0.05
c_max <- calibrate_c_max_aalen(lambda0     = lambda0,
                               beta        = beta,
                               prev        = prev, 
                               sigma       = sigma,
                               target_cens = target_cens,
                               seed        = 2026)
c_max
data <- simulate_aalen_data(n, prev, sigma, lambda0, beta, c_max)
head(data)

# Check prevalences
colMeans(data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("X")))

# Check censoring rate 
mean(1 - data$event)

# Kaplan-Meier 
km_aalen_fit <- survfit(Surv(time, event)~1, data)
km_aalen_fit
ggsurvplot(km_aalen_fit, data = data, legend = "none")
ggsurvplot(survfit(Surv(time, event)~X1, data), data)
ggsurvplot(survfit(Surv(time, event)~X2, data), data)
ggsurvplot(survfit(Surv(time, event)~X3, data), data)
ggsurvplot(survfit(Surv(time, event)~X4, data), data)

# Fit the Aalen model
# All components of the model have nonparametric time-varying effects.
aalen_fit <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+X3+X4, data = data)
summary(aalen_fit)

# Coefficients
# cum <- aalen_fit$cum
# tmax <- max(cum[, "time"])
# B_at_tmax <- cum[nrow(cum), -c(1,2)]
# est_beta <- as.vector(B_at_tmax / tmax)
# est_beta
  
# Plot the non-parametric cumulative regression coefficients with 95% CI
# par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(aalen_fit, ylim = c(-0.5,4))

# Fit the model with the effect of X3 and X4 being constant
aalen_fit2 <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~
                               X1+X2+const(X3)+const(X4), 
                             data = data)
summary(aalen_fit2)
# par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(aalen_fit2, ylim = c(-0.5,4))

# Fit the model with only time-constant effects 
aalen_fit3 <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~
                               const(X1)+const(X2)+const(X3)+const(X4), 
                             data = data)
summary(aalen_fit3)
plot(aalen_fit3, ylim = c(-0.5,4))
```

```{r}
R <- 1000
test_aalen <- replicate(R, {
  data <- simulate_aalen_data(n, prev, sigma, lambda0, beta, c_max)
  #'For constant effects: cumulative coefficient B_j(t) ≈ beta_j * t.
  #'Approximate beta_j by B_j(t_max) / t_max using the last available 
  #'cumulative estimate.
  #'timereg stores cumulative estimates in fit_obj$cum: matrix with columns 
  #'(time, (coef, se) per covariate).
  fit_obj <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+X3+X4, data)
  cum <- fit_obj$cum
  tmax <- max(cum[, "time"])
  B_at_tmax <- cum[nrow(cum), -c(1,2)]
  est_beta <- as.vector(B_at_tmax / tmax)
  
  data.frame(
    prev_X1   = mean(data$X1),
    prev_X2   = mean(data$X2),
    prev_X3   = mean(data$X3),
    prev_X4   = mean(data$X4),
    cens_rate = mean(1 - data$event),
    beta_X1   = est_beta[1],
    beta_X2   = est_beta[2],
    beta_X3   = est_beta[3],
    beta_X4   = est_beta[4]
    )}, simplify = F)

colMeans(bind_rows(test_aalen))
```

## Cox-Aalen model

The covariates are generated *independently* as follows:

$X = (X_{1}, X_{2}, X_{3}, X_{4})$ 

where
  
* $X_{1} \sim \mathcal{B}(0.4)$
* $X_{2} \sim \mathcal{B}(0.4)$
* $X_{3} \sim \mathcal{B}(0.4)$
* $X_{4} \sim \mathcal{N}(0,1)$

To generate mixed additive and multiplicative effects, event times were 
simulated under a Cox-Aalen model with constant baseline hazard: 
$\lambda_0(t)=0.05$. Additive effects were assigned to $X_1$ and $X_2$ with 
coefficients $\beta_{1}=0.06$ and $\beta_{3}=0.005$, respectively. 
Multiplicative (proportional hazards) effects were assigned to 
$X_3$ and $X_4$ with coefficents $\beta_{2}=0.5$ and
$\beta_{4}=0.2$, corresponding to hazard ratios of approximately 1.65 and 1.22. 

```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility 
set.seed(2026)

# Generate data
n <- 1000
prev_add <- c(0.4, 0.4)
sigma_add <- diag(x = 1, nrow = 2, ncol = 2)
beta_add <- c(0.06, 0.005)
prev_mult <- 0.4
sigma_mult_bin <- diag(x = 1, nrow = 1, ncol = 1)
mean_mult <- 0
sigma_mult_cont <- diag(x = 1, nrow = 1, ncol = 1)
beta_mult <- c(0.5, 0.2)
lambda0 <- 0.05
target_cens <- 0.05
c_max <- calibrate_c_max_cox_aalen(lambda0         = lambda0,
                                   beta_add        = beta_add,
                                   beta_mult       = beta_mult,
                                   prev_add        = prev_add,
                                   sigma_add       = sigma_add,
                                   prev_mult       = prev_mult,
                                   mean_mult       = mean_mult,
                                   sigma_mult_bin  = sigma_mult_bin,
                                   sigma_mult_cont = sigma_mult_cont,
                                   target_cens     = target_cens,
                                   seed            = 2026)
c_max
data <- simulate_cox_aalen_data(n, lambda0, beta_add, beta_mult,
                                prev_add, sigma_add,
                                prev_mult, mean_mult,
                                sigma_mult_bin, sigma_mult_cont,
                                c_max)
head(data)

# Check prevalences / mean 
colMeans(data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("X")))

# Check censoring rate 
mean(1 - data$event)

# Kaplan-Meier
km_cox_aalen_fit <- survfit(Surv(time, event)~1, data)
km_cox_aalen_fit
ggsurvplot(km_cox_aalen_fit, data = data, legend = "none")
ggsurvplot(survfit(Surv(time, event)~X1, data), data)
ggsurvplot(survfit(Surv(time, event)~X2, data), data)
ggsurvplot(survfit(Surv(time, event)~X3, data), data)

# Fit Cox-Aalen model 
cox_aalen_fit <- timereg::cox.aalen(
  Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+prop(X3)+prop(X4), 
  data)
summary(cox_aalen_fit)

# Plot additive Aalen terms
# par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plot(cox_aalen_fit, ylim = c(-0.5,5))

# Allow X2 to be time-constant 
cox_aalen_fit2 <- timereg::cox.aalen(
  Surv(time, event)~X1+const(X2)+prop(X3)+prop(X4), 
  data, robust = TRUE)
summary(cox_aalen_fit2)

# Proportional excess terms of model 
cox(cox_aalen_fit2)
# par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(cox_aalen_fit2,score= TRUE) 

# Plot additive Aalen terms
# par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plot(cox_aalen_fit2, ylim = c(-0.5, 5)) 
```
```{r}
R <- 1000
test_cox_aalen <- replicate(R, {
  data <- simulate_cox_aalen_data(n, lambda0, beta_add, beta_mult,
                                  prev_add, sigma_add,
                                  prev_mult, mean_mult,
                                  sigma_mult_bin, sigma_mult_cont,
                                  c_max)
  cox_aalen_fit <- timereg::cox.aalen(
    Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+prop(X3)+prop(X4),
    data = data)
  coef <- data.frame(coef(cox_aalen_fit))
  data.frame(
    prev_X1   = mean(data$X1),
    prev_X2   = mean(data$X2),
    prev_X3   = mean(data$X3),
    mean_X4   = mean(data$X4),
    cens_rate = mean(1 - data$event),
    beta_X3   = coef[1, "Coef."],
    beta_X4   = coef[2, "Coef."]
    )}, simplify = F)

colMeans(bind_rows(test_cox_aalen))
```
